rule_inferred: Input is a 2x2/2x3/3x2/3x3 array of colored noisy blocks separated by all-zero rows/columns. Output is the color ID of each block. Existing model computes first/last all-zero row/column and GridSamples representative cells.

attempts.name: static fixed GridSample 3x3
attempts.details: train/test visible partly passed in local require_false but arc-gen 37 pass / 128 fail; fresh 40/53 fail; fixed coordinates cannot follow variable separators.

attempts.name: one-axis static coordinate search
attempts.details: searched half/integer coordinates with dynamic other axis across 169 visible+arc-gen <=30 examples; no fixed row or fixed column triple exists.

attempts.name: MaxPool static window reconstruction
attempts.details: searched 5,820 MaxPool interval triples per axis (401 unique intervals); no static pooling windows satisfy all cells across 169 checked examples.

attempts.name: Pad raw one-hot after GridSample
attempts.details: background channel becomes positive when bilinear sample includes zeros; visible 0/4, arc-gen 21/165.

attempts.name: W float16 / W fp16 conversion
attempts.details: ONNX shape inference fails: Einsum inputs have inconsistent types.

conclusion: No valid lower-cost ONNX was found. In particular, no candidate achieved cost <=681 (baseline-50) while preserving visible/arc-gen/fresh correctness.
lower_bound_note: The current correct construction already pays 360 bytes for the 10-channel 3x3 sampled one-hot tensor plus 240 bytes for row/column score vectors; the remaining grid/coordinate/control tensors and params bring the measured cost to 731.
